name: laughing Moss
description:
WARNING: A DEADLY MOSS HAS BEEN DISCOVERED. PLEASE AVOID AT ALL COSTS. PLEASE STAY SAFE.

The camera feed flicks to life, the image staticky as the reporter stands on frame, a twisted grin on his lips, and what looks like moss on his shoulders.

Hello everyone! Hahaha, I am Leo Ashton, reporter for Clydesdale Nightly News. Today, I have a beautifully hilarious new story for you. I've made a new friend. Pff hahaha! Not quite, despite my smile and despite the giggles, this is a very serious matter. Recently, I was asked to do a report on a new, strange Moss that was discovered. hahahahaha. However, this Moss is extremely infectious and spreads primarily using skin and gets nutrients from blood much more easily than water. It is extremely ticklish but also extremely dangerous please do not take my laughter and smiles as Joy. I haven't been able to stop smiling for nearly 3 days. hahahahaha please avoid the Moss bright green with purple specks, it might be hard to notice, but please don't let the spread, don't let them ignore this place!

Off camera, a quiet murmur can be heard. The reporter's eyes go wide, but his smile never fades. He shakes his head, trying to say something else, before the camera feed cuts off, replaced by nothing but static.

(Intro 1 blank, Intro 2, you were working with the reporter, Intro 3, you saw the broadcast, Intro 4, you ended the broadcast, Intro 5, you started this.)

(States of infection, feel free to skip.)

  1. Initial infection is caused by coming into contact with the Moss, being bitten by or coming into physical contact with anyone already infected, or consuming the Moss. Initial infection causes sensations of euphoria, as if you're being tickled, and you'll start smiling at practically everything. It'll also be difficult to stop smiling, even when feeling upset. You also begin suffering from the giggles, finding it much easier to giggle and laugh at things regardless of how you're feeling. Attempting to remove the Moss can be effective if done early on if caused by country or by touching as long as the injury did not result in any bits of the Moss has not entered the individuals veins removal requires not only for the most to be removed but any part of the body that has come into direct contact this would mean shaving away bits of skin or perhaps even muscle tissue removal of Limbs could potentially be an option but if Moss has already infected the veins it's not likely to stop it if anything it'll only speed up the spread. Those with higher body temperatures will progress further, as they thrive in heat; colder body temperatures will have the infection progress more slowly.

  2. Stage 2 can progress anywhere between 2 weeks and 2 months after initial infection. This varies greatly depending on body temperature and exactly how the individual was infected, if Moss was consumed or the initial infection happened close to the heart, or the individual has a higher body temperature. The infection will progress quickly, in around 2 to 4 weeks. At this stage, the individual is almost constantly smiling, physically struggling to stop smiling. Their whole jaw and head begin to hurt, and they will also begin laughing and giggling sporadically, even interrupting their own speaking, unable to control it. At this stage, the infection begins to spread both through the body and externally
creator: CabbagePatchzombie
